did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper proposes MSCAM-Net, a deep learning model using an EfficientNet-B2 backbone, Enhanced Feature Extraction Module (EFEM), Context-Aware Feature Aggregation (CAFA), and Adaptive Fusion Module (AFM) for skin lesion segmentation. It was trained and evaluated on the ISIC 2017 and ISIC 2018 dermoscopic image datasets to segment malignant melanoma lesions.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper presents MSCAM-Net, a novel deep learning model for skin lesion segmentation, including its architecture (EFEM, CAFA, AFM), implementation details (EfficientNet-B2 backbone, PyTorch, ISIC 2017/2018 datasets), and experimental results (Dice/IoU metrics). It reports original experimental findings and comparative analysis, classifying it as a research paper. Although published in the 2025 IEEE ISBI proceedings, it contains full original research content rather than being merely an abstract.

title: MSCAM-NET: A MULTI-SCALE AND CONTEXT-AWARE MODEL WITH ADAPTIVE FUSION MODULE FOR ROBUST SKIN LESION SEGMENTATION
journal: 2025 IEEE 22nd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
brief_summary: This study proposes MSCAM-Net, a novel deep learning model for precise skin lesion segmentation in dermoscopic images to aid in malignant melanoma detection. The architecture integrates an EfficientNet-B2 backbone with an Enhanced Feature Extraction Module (EFEM), Context-Aware Feature Aggregation (CAFA), and an Adaptive Fusion Module (AFM) to capture multi-scale features and dynamic context. The model was trained and evaluated on the ISIC 2017 and ISIC 2018 datasets using Dice coefficient and Intersection over Union (IoU) metrics. Experimental results demonstrate that MSCAM-Net achieves superior performance, reaching a Dice score of 0.931 and IoU of 0.858 on ISIC 2017, and 0.914 Dice and 0.825 IoU on ISIC 2018. These results surpass recent state-of-the-art methods, including UltraLight VM-UNet, confirming the model's effectiveness for robust skin lesion segmentation.

lesion_types_studied.details: The study focuses on segmenting malignant melanoma lesions using the ISIC 2017 and ISIC 2018 datasets, which are described as skin lesion segmentation datasets. The paper explicitly states the goal is to aid in malignant melanoma detection and segmentation.

all_task_types.target: Pixel-wise binary mask of skin lesions (malignant melanoma) in dermoscopic images
all_task_types.details: The paper explicitly focuses on 'skin lesion segmentation' using the ISIC 2017 and ISIC 2018 datasets. The model produces a predicted probability map (after sigmoid activation) for binary segmentation, optimized with Tversky Loss and Weighted Binary Cross-Entropy Loss. Performance is evaluated using Dice coefficient and Intersection over Union (IoU) metrics, which are standard for segmentation tasks.

datasets_used.name: ISIC 2017
datasets_used.description: A publicly available skin lesion segmentation dataset comprising dermoscopic images, hosted by the International Skin Imaging Collaboration (ISIC).

use.training: Used for training the MSCAM-Net model (80% of the dataset).
use.validation: Used for validation of the MSCAM-Net model (20% of the dataset).
use.testing: Used for testing and evaluation of the MSCAM-Net model.

datasets_used.name: ISIC 2018
datasets_used.description: A publicly available skin lesion segmentation dataset comprising dermoscopic images, hosted by the International Skin Imaging Collaboration (ISIC).

use.training: Used for training the MSCAM-Net model (80% of the dataset).
use.validation: Used for validation of the MSCAM-Net model (20% of the dataset).
use.testing: Used for testing and evaluation of the MSCAM-Net model.

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report any formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. Performance comparisons were made using absolute metric values (Dice coefficient and IoU) and percentage improvements over baseline models.

ISIC 2017.notes: MSCAM-Net surpassed VM-UNet by 1.7% in Dice and 2.1% in IoU.

ISIC 2018.notes: MSCAM-Net exceeded VM-UNet by 1% in Dice and 1.7% in IoU.

architecture_details.efem_details: Captures multi-scale features using convolutions of different kernel sizes, followed by batch normalization, ReLU activation, and a 1x1 convolution to reduce channel dimension.
architecture_details.cafa_details: Enriches features with global context using large-kernel convolution and global average pooling, followed by upsampling and residual connection.
architecture_details.afm_details: Adaptively fuses features from different convolutional scales using learnable weights (alpha, beta, gamma) via batch normalization and ReLU activation.
architecture_details.refinement_block: Double Depthwise Separable Convolution (DSC) layers with batch normalization and ReLU activation, with skip connections.
architecture_details.decoder: Dynamic upsampling using ConvTranspose2d, fusing with encoder features via AFM.
architecture_details.auxiliary_outputs: Deep supervision with auxiliary outputs at intermediate stages via 1x1 convolution.

training_details.hardware: NVIDIA RTX 3060 GPU with 6 GB memory
training_details.loss_function: Combined Tversky Loss and Weighted Binary Cross-Entropy Loss (WBCE) with auxiliary losses.

preprocessing_techniques: Data augmentation: horizontal and vertical flipping
Data augmentation: brightness and contrast adjustments

conclusions: MSCAM-Net effectively captures multi-scale and context-aware features, enhancing segmentation accuracy on complex skin lesions.
MSCAM-Net achieves superior segmentation performance compared to recent state-of-the-art methods, including UltraLight VM-UNet, on the ISIC 2017 and ISIC 2018 datasets.
On the ISIC 2017 dataset, MSCAM-Net achieved a Dice score of 0.931 and IoU of 0.858, surpassing VM-UNet by 1.7% in Dice and 2.1% in IoU.
On the ISIC 2018 dataset, MSCAM-Net achieved a Dice score of 0.914 and IoU of 0.825, exceeding VM-UNet by 1% in Dice and 1.7% in IoU.

The integration of the Enhanced Feature Extraction Module (EFEM), Context-Aware Feature Aggregation (CAFA), and Adaptive Fusion Module (AFM) significantly improves feature learning and segmentation accuracy.
MSCAM-Net demonstrates potential for clinical application in melanoma diagnosis and treatment due to its robust performance on dermoscopic images.
important_remarks: The study relies exclusively on the ISIC 2017 and ISIC 2018 datasets, which may limit generalizability to other populations or imaging devices due to potential dataset bias.
No external validation was performed; the model was only evaluated on the two ISIC datasets it was trained on.
The paper does not report any statistical significance tests (e.g., p-values) for the performance improvements over baseline models.
The model was trained on a single NVIDIA RTX 3060 GPU with 6 GB memory, which may limit scalability or reproducibility for researchers with different hardware constraints.
The paper claims potential for clinical application but provides no evidence of clinical workflow integration, user studies, or real-world deployment testing.
The code is publicly available (https://github.com/Zumnan/MSCAM-Net), which supports reproducibility.
The model uses an 80:20 random split for training and validation, which may introduce variance depending on the specific split; no cross-validation results are reported.
